title: Revert "Support Windows11 As A sshuttle Server"
body:
Reverts sshuttle/sshuttle#993

This change was made in error. See discussion in #993.

step_plan.summary: Revert the erroneous Windows11-remote-server support by removing Windows-specific remote bootstrap/stdio shims in the SSH connector and eliminating the Windows-only runtime path in the server entrypoint, restoring the prior POSIX-only remote server behavior.

steps.operation: Revert Windows-specific remote server bootstrap and transport shims in SSH connection setup (remove/restore win32 branches and cmd/powershell remote-shell launching so remote server is treated as POSIX-only again)
steps.reason: PR #993 introduced Windows-server enablement primarily via Windows-specific stdio/mux shims (e.g., SocketRWShim + pipe/socket bridging) and remote-shell modes intended to start the remote server under cmd/powershell. Reverting that support means restoring the pre-#993 behavior: build a POSIX-oriented remote python launcher and use the standard SSH stdio wiring expected by the mux protocol.
steps.side_effects: May break workflows that relied on Windows being the *remote* server (intended). Must ensure Windows-as-client still works (local platform may still be win32). Any callers that pass remote_shell=cmd/powershell may now error or fall back; update help/validation accordingly. Re-run integration paths that establish a remote server over SSH to confirm no regression on Linux/macOS servers.

steps.operation: Remove Windows-server runtime path in server entrypoint (eliminate win32 mux initialization/deferred-exit workaround and restore single, POSIX-oriented stdin/stdout mux handling)
steps.reason: Windows-specific server.main() logic (SocketRWShim wrapping of stdin/stdout and deferred exit handling) exists specifically to let sshuttle.server run on Windows. Reverting #993 requires removing that runtime support so the server component assumes a POSIX-like environment and uses the original mux IO behavior.
steps.side_effects: Running sshuttle.server on Windows will no longer be supported (intended). Ensure the mux protocol behavior remains unchanged for POSIX servers; verify shutdown/exit semantics remain correct without the deferred-exit workaround. Any tests/fixtures that executed server.main() on win32 must be removed/adjusted.
